level_signals: Owns full feature roadmap for Protect product
Partners with data scientists and MLEs as a day-to-day collaborator
Requires hands-on fraud prevention experience and direct DS/ML collaboration experience

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Real hands-on fraud prevention experience: fraud vectors, detection systems, risk signal evaluation. Not general fintech.

Direct experience collaborating with data scientists or ML engineers to ship data-driven products. You need to be able to talk to a DS about feature distributions, label quality, and model performance, not just read summaries.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience at a fraud vendor (SentiLink, Sardine, Socure, Persona, Incode) or a fraud operations team at a large fintech or bank
employer_types_named: fraud operations team at a large fintech or bank

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Background with ML model inputs/outputs: feature engineering, offline evaluation, and moving from experimentation to production
Specialization in a specific fraud vector (ATO, synthetic identity, or first-party fraud)

explicitly_excluded: General fintech experience (without specific fraud prevention focus)

scope.surface: Protect — Plaid's fraud intelligence platform; specifically the signal stack (attributes, scores, fraud vector intelligence) and labeling pipeline
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS / fintech infrastructure — fraud data products sold to enterprise and fintech customers
primary_metrics_inferred: Precision/recall targets on fraud vector scores
Label quality and feedback loop effectiveness
Customer-facing value from attribute improvements and new scores
core_responsibilities: Own the feature roadmap for Protect: what we build, how we validate it, and how we measure coverage and fill rates
Build and manage the labeling pipeline as a product, including customer retros, data partnerships, and the label feedback loop into model training
Define the product requirements for fraud vector scores, including signal selection, precision/recall targets, and customer-facing output design
Partner with data scientists and MLEs as a day-to-day collaborator, not just a requester. Translate between model needs and product requirements in both directions
Work with GTM and Protect PM on how attribute improvements and new scores translate into customer-facing value propositions

green_flags: Highly specific and well-scoped product role — clear ownership area (Protect signal stack)
Explicit differentiation between required and nice-to-have qualifications
JD is technically precise, signaling a sophisticated hiring team
Encourages applications even from candidates who don't fully match
Equity mentioned as part of compensation package
Medical, dental, vision, and 401(k) benefits explicitly stated
Plaid is an established fintech infrastructure company with significant market presence (12,000 FIs)
red_flags: No explicit location or remote policy stated — unclear if role is hybrid, onsite, or remote
Hard requirement for hands-on fraud experience specifically excludes general fintech backgrounds — narrows candidate pool significantly
No years of experience stated, making seniority level ambiguous

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from scope of ownership and DS/ML collaboration requirements; no explicit level or years stated)
